note: Crafts of the Somatic Reaches/Cogitarium (body_mind axis). Soma is the body-pole tradition (the physical masteries, the body as instrument); Noesis the mind-pole (mental disciplines, memory-palaces, thought as force). Learned by traveling the Reach or training with Somatics/Cogitants. Both drift the wielder toward their pole; the perfected athlete-scholar is where they co-fire, the disembodied Cogitant where mind wins absolutely. axes signed for engine compat. Authored by Aevi (PO), SNG-016 — the last of the six near-axis traditions.

abilities.name: Soma
abilities.narrationHints: The body made instrument — breath, poise, endurance past what should be possible, movement as a language spoken without words.
abilities.description: The body-pole mastery: breath-control, endurance, poise, the physical self honed to an instrument. The Flesh-Temples' art.
abilities.notFor: Thought-work or memory (that's Noesis); brute force alone (Soma is control, not just strength); anything requiring you to leave the body behind.

tree.name: The Trained Body
tree.grants: Endurance and poise past the ordinary; control breath and heartbeat to steady fear, pain, or cold; move with a precision that reads as grace.
tree.cannot: The body still has limits; Soma raises the ceiling, it doesn't remove it.

tree.name: The Instrument
tree.grants: Feats of body that look impossible — hold a bridge-pose an hour, run past collapse, slow your own bleeding, take a blow and keep the form; teach the body a skill in days that takes others years.
tree.cannot: Pushing past the true limit still breaks you; the body remembers what you spend.

tree.name: The Perfected Vessel
tree.grants: The body as a mastered whole — survive what kills others, perform at the edge of the humanly possible, make the physical self answer the will completely. AND THE BODY AS THE INSTRUMENT IT IS: a strike thrown with the whole mastered frame behind it — stance, breath, and timing arriving together — wounds far past what the same arm could do untrained.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts body-hot — and the Temple that rejects thought forgets to warn itself; a perfected body with a starved mind is its own foreclosure, mastery with nothing left to aim it.

abilities.name: Noesis
abilities.narrationHints: Thought made a discipline and a force — memory-palaces, absolute focus, reasoning that outruns the room, the mind holding what the body can't.
abilities.description: The mind-pole mastery: memory, focus, reason, thought as an instrument sharp enough to act on the world. The Cogitarium's art.
abilities.notFor: Physical feats (that's Soma); wisdom (Noesis is sharp, not necessarily wise); anything requiring presence in the body it keeps trying to leave.

tree.name: The Ordered Mind
tree.grants: Perfect recall of what you attend to; focus that ignores pain, fear, and distraction; reason a problem faster and further than the unpracticed.
tree.cannot: Recall is not understanding; focus this tight can miss the body's warnings.

tree.name: The Memory-Palace
tree.grants: Hold vast structured knowledge and retrieve it instantly; model a system or a person in your head and predict them; think through a crisis in the space others panic in.
tree.cannot: The model is only as good as its inputs; a mind that lives in the model loses the room.

tree.name: The Cogitant's Height
tree.grants: Thought as force — solve the unsolvable, hold a hundred threads at once, out-think a situation so completely you seem to move before it does. And turned on a person: a conclusion assembled and delivered exactly where their reasoning rests, which staggers a mind the way a blow staggers a body, and leaves the same kind of mark.
tree.cannot: It is sharp, not wise, and this use of it least of all. It needs a mind that reasons — the disciplined, the genuinely mad, and the simply uninterested all shrug it off. And the Cogitarium teaches that what you take apart in someone this way, you are responsible for putting back.

abilities.name: Body-Read
abilities.narrationHints: A person's health, strength, and hurt read from how they stand.
abilities.description: Read a body — its condition, hidden injury, strength, exhaustion, tells.

tree.name: Body-Read
tree.grants: Read another's physical condition, hidden hurts, and capacities at a glance.
tree.cannot: Body, not mind; the trained conceal tells.

tree.name: The Whole Tell
tree.grants: Read the whole body at once — weight, breath, the held shoulder, what the hands are doing when the face is busy lying.
tree.cannot: It reads the body's report. A trained body reports what its owner chooses.

tree.name: The Body Cannot Lie
tree.grants: Nothing a body does escapes you. Pain, intent, love, the decision made a half-second before it is acted on.
tree.cannot: You cannot stop reading. The Somatics say knowing every body in a room is its own kind of loneliness.

abilities.name: Cold-Read
abilities.narrationHints: The turn of a mind inferred from a hundred small signs.
abilities.description: Infer another's thoughts and intent from micro-signals — not magic, mastery of tells.

tree.name: Cold-Read
tree.grants: Deduce a person's likely thoughts and next move from their signals.
tree.cannot: Inference, not certainty; masters and non-humans resist.

tree.name: The Cold Line
tree.grants: Follow a mind's reasoning as it happens — where it is going, what it has already ruled out.
tree.cannot: You follow the reasoning, not the reasons. A mind moving on feeling is opaque to a cold line.

tree.name: The Open Book
tree.grants: A mind at rest is legible to you — its shape, its habits, the thing it keeps returning to.
tree.cannot: The Cogitants consider unbidden reading a violence, and they are right, and they do it anyway.

abilities.name: Second Wind
abilities.narrationHints: Past the wall, and the body finds more.
abilities.description: Push the body past exhaustion, pain, or shock for a burst — folk-Soma.

tree.name: Second Wind
tree.grants: Ignore pain, fatigue, or shock for a short, decisive burst of physical effort.
tree.cannot: The body remembers the debt; overuse breaks you.

tree.name: The Third Wind
tree.grants: Find breath and strength past where the body had none — twice, in one day, at a cost. And what you find is not simply more of what you had: for as long as it lasts you are stronger and faster than your own rested self, which is exactly why it costs what it costs.
tree.cannot: The body is borrowing from tomorrow, and tomorrow collects with interest.

tree.name: The Wind That Doesn't End
tree.grants: Go on past exhaustion for as long as the thing needs doing — the body simply does not stop. And it tends itself as it runs: bleeding slows, shock recedes, the worst of the damage held off until the work is finished.
tree.cannot: Held off is not healed — everything deferred arrives at once when you stop, and it arrives worse. This is borrowing, at a rate the Flesh-Temples consider usurious.

abilities.name: Total Focus
abilities.narrationHints: The world narrows to the one thing, and nothing reaches you.
abilities.description: Enter absolute focus — ignore fear, distraction, and pain to think or work clean. Folk-Noesis.

tree.name: Total Focus
tree.grants: Focus so completely that fear, pain, and distraction fall away while you think or work.
tree.cannot: Can miss the body's warnings; tight focus narrows the view.

tree.name: The Narrowed World
tree.grants: Narrow to one thing so completely that fear, pain, and noise stop reaching you.
tree.cannot: What you narrow out is still happening. People have been focused to death.

tree.name: The Single Point
tree.grants: Narrow to one thing so completely that nothing else exists — not fear, not pain, not the wound you are standing on. And within that narrowing the thing opens completely: every detail of it available at once, because nothing at all is competing for the attention. The task is completed.
tree.cannot: You will not notice what it cost until afterward. Cogitants have finished the task and then simply fallen over.

abilities.name: Perfect Motion
abilities.narrationHints: The body does exactly and only what's needed, flawlessly.
abilities.description: Execute a physical feat with impossible precision and economy — mid-tier Soma.
abilities.notFor: Feats past the body's true ceiling; thought-work.

tree.name: Perfect Motion
tree.grants: Perform a physical act at the very edge of the possible — balance, speed, precision, control.
tree.cannot: The true limit still holds; costs the body dearly.

tree.name: The Unwasted Motion
tree.grants: No motion you make is wasted — every step, block, and reach does exactly and only what it must.
tree.cannot: Efficiency is not power. A perfect motion still loses to a stronger one.

tree.name: Motion Without Thought
tree.grants: The body acts correctly before the mind knows there was a decision — and it is right.
tree.cannot: The body chooses. If you have trained it badly, it will do that beautifully.

abilities.name: Memory-Palace
abilities.narrationHints: Vast knowledge held in a built inner architecture, retrieved instantly.
abilities.description: Hold and instantly retrieve vast structured knowledge; model a person or system in the mind — mid-tier Noesis.
abilities.notFor: Wisdom; presence in the body it forgets.

tree.name: Memory-Palace
tree.grants: Store and recall huge structured knowledge; model and predict a system or person in your head.
tree.cannot: Only as good as its inputs; living in the model loses the room.

tree.name: The Deeper Rooms
tree.grants: Store not just facts but experiences whole — walk back into a conversation and hear it again exactly.
tree.cannot: What you store, you keep. Including what you would rather have let fade.

tree.name: The Palace Entire
tree.grants: Nothing you have attended to is lost. The whole of your life is a place you can walk through.
tree.cannot: The Cogitants who build the deepest palaces have trouble leaving them.

abilities.name: The Whole Act
abilities.narrationHints: Thought and motion stop being two things.
abilities.description: The braid-craft: body and mind as one instrument, acting faster than either could decide — the Middle Way's ideal.
abilities.notFor: Body-heavy or mind-heavy wielders (needs both near-equal); the extremes.

tree.name: The Whole Act
tree.grants: Act with body and mind fused — perception, decision, and motion as a single instant.
tree.cannot: Needs both trained near-equal; the pure poles cannot do it.

tree.name: The Unbroken Act
tree.grants: Hold the fusion across a whole engagement rather than a single instant. Every movement in the exchange is one continuous act — you are already correct before you have consciously read why, and you stay correct as the situation changes under you.
tree.cannot: It still needs both trained near-equal and the pure poles still cannot do it; holding it leaves you unable to afterward explain what you did or why, which matters when someone asks; and a whole act cannot be interrupted and resumed — broken, it must be re-entered from stillness.

abilities.name: The Pathless Way
abilities.narrationHints: The unclimbable is climbed, the uncrossable crossed — the body finds a way where there is none.
abilities.description: The traversal capstone: cross terrain that stops everyone else — sheer faces, killing cold, drowning fords, the deep dark — by mastery of the body pushed past every ordinary limit.
abilities.notFor: Teleportation or flight (it's mastery, not magic); carrying others easily; ignoring true lethality unwarned.

tree.name: The Pathless Way
tree.grants: Cross the uncrossable — any natural barrier yields to enough skill, breath, and will; lead a party through what would kill them alone.
tree.cannot: The truly impossible (a sheer mile of ice in a storm) still exacts a price; you can fail, spectacularly, and the fall is real.

abilities.name: Skydancer
abilities.narrationHints: The body treats the air like ground — a leap becomes a bound becomes something the earth stopped being able to stop.
abilities.description: Flight-by-mastery: prodigious leaps, wall-runs, glides, and air-steps that keep you aloft in bursts. Not true hovering — a body so trained the air briefly holds it. Cross rooftops, chasms, close on a fleeing foe from above.
abilities.notFor: Sustained hovering or true flight (it's bursts, momentum-bound); carrying heavy loads aloft.

tree.name: Skydancer
tree.grants: Move through the air in bounds, glides, and wall-runs — cross gaps, scale heights, drop on a target.
tree.cannot: Momentum-bound (you must keep moving, no hover); a mistimed step falls; heavy burden grounds you.

tree.name: The Long Air
tree.grants: Chain the air-steps without touching down — cross a canyon, ascend a tower face in one unbroken sequence, hold the air long enough to choose your landing. And you may carry another person through it with you.
tree.cannot: Still momentum-bound; there is no hover at any mastery, only better momentum. Carrying another halves your reach and a mistimed step now falls two people. Heavy burden still grounds you outright.

abilities.name: The Working Model
abilities.narrationHints: Think it through until it builds itself. A plan become a blueprint become a thing.
abilities.description: You can think a thing into existence — model it so completely in your mind that the making is just following the thought.

tree.name: Blueprint
tree.grants: Think through a construction or modification so precisely that the making is near-guaranteed, even with inferior tools; identify exactly what you need before you touch anything.

tree.name: The Complete Model
tree.grants: Build a mental model of a complex system — a machine, a building, a body — complete enough to diagnose its faults and redesign its structure from the inside out.

tree.name: Think-Built
tree.grants: A thing you have modeled completely you can build with uncanny precision and speed — faster and more accurately than available materials or tools should allow.

abilities.name: The Necessary Case
abilities.narrationHints: Not persuasion. Demonstration. The conclusion is the only place the logic goes.
abilities.description: You present a case so airtight that refusal becomes irrational. Not manipulation — proof. The other person can disagree, but they cannot explain why.

tree.name: Closed Argument
tree.grants: Present a case so logically complete that the other person cannot find a gap — not because you've overwhelmed them, but because there isn't one.

tree.name: Demonstrated Necessity
tree.grants: Show that a conclusion follows so inevitably that the person cannot act against it without explaining why — and you can dismantle whatever they offer.

tree.name: The Only Conclusion
tree.grants: Make the logic of a situation so visible that a group reorganizes around it — not because you commanded them, but because the structure of the situation leaves no other rational position.

abilities.name: The Clear Path
abilities.narrationHints: The gap is there before you look. The model says where to step.
abilities.description: Navigate by modeling everything — obstacles, crowd, guard rotation — before you move a foot. The path is solved before it is walked.

tree.name: Modeled Route
tree.grants: Find the quickest, safest route through any observable space — crowd, ruin, wilderness — by calculating rather than feeling your way.

tree.name: Anticipatory Movement
tree.grants: Move through a dynamic space without friction by staying two steps ahead of everyone in it — the guard, the crowd, the chaos.

tree.name: The Written Choreography
tree.grants: In a space you understand, you move as if you designed the whole scene — every obstacle parts, every gap opens, every opening appears the instant you need it.

abilities.name: The Long Form
abilities.narrationHints: The problem is still there. So is the mind working it.
abilities.description: Mental work that doesn't stop — held across days, against exhaustion, against everything that says to let go.

tree.name: Maintained Function
tree.grants: Hold a complex train of thought through interruption, fear, pain, or deprivation; return to exactly where you were without loss.

tree.name: Extended Work
tree.grants: Sustain complex cognitive work — a problem, a plan, a translation — across days without the thinking degrading, even as sleep and food grow scarce.

tree.name: The Mind That Holds
tree.grants: Your mind is simply the last thing to fail. Function clearly under conditions that have broken better bodies — cold, injury, grief, weeks of pressure — until the work is done.

abilities.name: The Shaped Body
abilities.narrationHints: Train the body past what the body thinks it can be. A new configuration, held.
abilities.description: Reshape a physical capacity through extreme, focused training — not temporary effort, but permanent change in what the body is capable of.

tree.name: Focused Adaptation
tree.grants: Through extreme, focused training, significantly alter one specific physical capacity — grip strength, pain threshold, cold tolerance, sprinting burst — in ways ordinary training doesn't reach.

tree.name: New Configuration
tree.grants: Reshape something structural about how you move across weeks of sustained effort — a different gait, a changed shoulder set, a fundamental adaptation that the body now holds.

tree.name: The Body Is Clay
tree.grants: You have worked your body long enough to know it as material. Any physical capacity you turn full, sustained attention to becomes, in time, what you need it to be.

abilities.name: The Weight of Presence
abilities.narrationHints: Full inhabitation of a body has a gravity. Other bodies feel it before the mind does.
abilities.description: Your physical presence is difficult to ignore or act against — not threat, but the authority of someone completely at home in their body.

tree.name: Present Weight
tree.grants: Your physical presence in a space is difficult to ignore — people make room, the uneasy grow more so, those considering action against you reconsider.

tree.name: The Organizing Fact
tree.grants: Command a space through presence alone — enter a room and it restructures; stand still and the situation organizes itself relative to where you are.

tree.name: Immovable Ground
tree.grants: Your presence is the fixed fact in a room. Anyone who would act against you must first account for what your body represents — and most find they cannot.

abilities.name: The Unmoving Mind
abilities.narrationHints: Everything they throw at your thoughts slides off a stillness that was there before they arrived and will be there after they tire. You did not brace. You were simply already still.
abilities.description: The Cogitant art of mushin — no-mind, the perfect stillness that nothing can move: not fear, not deception, not the fastest attack, because a mind that does not flinch reads everything and grants nothing. The blade-discipline made total.
abilities.notFor: It is defense and clarity, never force — you cannot strike with stillness, only see and endure. And a mind held perfectly still holds nothing else; while you are unmoving you are only unmoving.

tree.name: The Unmoving Mind
tree.grants: Enter a perfect mental stillness — immune to fear, deception, and disruption, reading all with total clarity.
tree.cannot: It grants no offense, only the unshakeable read; it occupies you wholly to hold; and a stillness this deep can look, to those who love you, like you have gone somewhere they cannot follow.

tree.name: The Moving Stillness
tree.grants: The stillness no longer occupies you wholly — hold it while fighting, walking, negotiating, and the clarity holds with it. And it reaches past you: those who stand with you and trust you are steadied by proximity, and do not break when they should.
tree.cannot: Extending it thins it — the more you shelter, the less clearly you read. It still grants no offense at any depth. And those sheltered feel the flinch you are not having, which more than one person has found worse than their own fear.

abilities.name: The Single Stroke
abilities.narrationHints: You have already seen it — the one moment, the only opening, the single stroke that ends the contest before it was properly a contest. You do not rush. You wait for the instant you already know is coming, and then it simply is.
abilities.description: The Cogitant capstone — mind and act become one perfect stroke, the whole contest read to its single decisive instant and then delivered without waste. The sword-saint's art: not the fastest blade, the ONLY necessary one.
abilities.notFor: One stroke, read and delivered perfectly, at enormous cost of focus — and it demands the opening be real; against something with no decisive instant (a flood, a crowd, a lie with no center) there is no single stroke to find.

tree.name: The Single Stroke
tree.grants: Read a contest to its one decisive instant and deliver the single perfect stroke that ends it.
tree.cannot: It costs nearly all your focus, it requires a real decisive instant to exist, and it is the blade's truth and the pen's alike — some contests have no single stroke, and mistaking that is how the sure-handed fall.
